zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Andrew Stewart Esq.於大約1244年出生在英國蘇格蘭艾斯爾歐文時，他的父親Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland31歲，母親Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness36歲。他於大約1299年與Miss Bethe在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1350年在他的家鄉享年108歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Andrew Stewart Esq.于大约1244年出生在英国苏格兰艾斯爾歐文时，他的父亲Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland31岁，母亲Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness36岁。他于大约1299年与Miss Bethe在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于大约1350年在他的家乡享年108岁。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Andrew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the Greek name Andreas, a short form of any of various compound names derived from andr- ‘man, warrior’. In the New Testament this is the name of the first disciple to be called by Jesus. After the Resurrection, St Andrew preached in Asia Minor and Greece. He is traditionally believed to have been crucified at Patras in Achaia. He was one of the most popular saints of the Middle Ages and was adopted as the patron of Scotland, Russia, and Greece. It has long been among the most popular boys' names in the English-speaking world, especially in Scotland. Its popularity in England was further enhanced by its use as a British royal name for Prince Andrew ( b. 1960 ), the Duke of York.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Andrew Stewart Esq. was born about 1244, in Irvine, Ayrshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland, was 31 and his mother, Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness, was 36. He married Miss Bethe about 1299, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died about 1350, in his hometown, at the age of 108.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Andrew Stewart Esq. est né environ 1244, à Irvine, Ayrshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness, avait 36 ans. Il a épousé Miss Bethe environ 1299, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé environ 1350, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 108 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Andrew Stewart Esq. um 1244 in Irvine, Ayrshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland 31 und seine Mutter Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness 36. Er hat um 1299 in England, United Kingdom Miss Bethe geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb um 1350 im Alter von 108 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Andrew Stewart Esq. è nato il circa 1244 nel Irvine, Ayrshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness, aveva 36 anni. Lui sposato Miss Bethe circa 1299, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì circa 1350, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 108 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Andrew Stewart Esq.が約1244年にイギリススコットランドエイルシュアイアイルヴィンで生まれた時，父Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotlandは31歳，母Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchionessは36歳であった。約1299年にイギリスイギリスでMiss Betheと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は約1350年に故郷で108歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Andrew Stewart Esq.(이)가 대략 1244년 영국 스코틀랜드 에이르실르 이르빈에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness(은)는 36세였다. 그는 대략 1299년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Miss Bethe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1350년 그의 고향에서 108세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Andrew Stewart Esq. родился около 1244 г. в местности ирваин, аиршир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Alexander Stewart 4th High Steward Of Scotland было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Jean MacRory Countess Bute Marchioness, 36. Он женился на женщине по имени Miss Bethe около 1299 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер около 1350 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 108 лет.
